summary: Original Retrieval-Augmented Generation combining retrieval with seq2seq generation. Foundational work.
Bi-encoder dense retrieval replacing BM25 for question answering.
Late interaction retrieval with token-level matching. Foundation for DSPy creator's retrieval work.
Hypothetical Document Embeddings - generate hypothetical answer then retrieve similar real docs.
Self-reflective RAG that trains LM to adaptively retrieve, generate, and critique. ICLR 2024.
Recursive abstractive processing for tree-organized retrieval at multiple granularities. ICLR 2024.
Microsoft graph-based RAG using knowledge graphs with community summaries. 31.8K GitHub stars.
Simple and fast graph-based RAG from HKUDS. EMNLP 2025. 31.2K stars.
Paradigm embedding autonomous AI agents into RAG pipeline with reflection, planning, tool use.
Hierarchical retrieval interfaces for agentic RAG with keyword, semantic, chunk read tools. Feb 2026.
Retrieval evaluation and correction mechanism for RAG. Details need verification.
Routes between different retrieval strategies based on query complexity.
Decomposes RAG into interchangeable modules for flexible pipeline construction.
Created ColBERT and DSPy. Stanford. Key figure in retrieval and programmatic LLM frameworks.
First author of original RAG paper. Meta/UCL.
Self-RAG author. University of Washington.
Leading open-source RAG engine with agent capabilities. 76.6K GitHub stars. By Infiniflow.
Most popular LLM application framework with comprehensive RAG tooling.
Data framework for LLM applications, strong RAG focus with sophisticated indexing.
Programmatic framework replacing prompt engineering. By Omar Khattab, Stanford.
Open-source AI orchestration framework by deepset. 24.6K stars. Modular pipelines.
All-in-one RAG framework from HKUDS. Multi-modal document processing. 14.7K stars.
Production-ready agentic RAG with RESTful API. 7.7K stars.
AutoML-style RAG evaluation and optimization framework. 4.6K stars.
Neuroscience-inspired RAG with knowledge graphs and personalized PageRank. NeurIPS 2024.
Integrated RAG platform on Langflow + Docling + Opensearch. 3.6K stars. New 2025.
Simple retrieve-then-generate single pass. Baseline approach.
Pre-retrieval optimization + post-retrieval reranking pipeline.
Combining sparse (BM25) + dense retrieval for better recall and precision.
Cross-encoder reranking after initial retrieval for precision improvement.
Fixed-size, semantic, recursive, late chunking, contextual retrieval. Key engineering decision.
Rewriting, decomposition, step-back prompting, HyDE for better retrieval.
Iterative retrieval for complex multi-step questions.
Emerging paradigm for dynamically assembling optimal context. Successor to naive RAG thinking.
LLMs struggle with information in middle of long contexts. Affects both RAG and long-context.
Irrelevant retrieved documents degrade generation quality.
Not either/or. RAG more cost-efficient, long context better with ample resources. Context Engineering emerging.

entity: Chunking: no consensus on optimal strategy
summary: Adaptive chunking 87% vs fixed 13% in clinical study but recursive 512 beats semantic in general benchmarks.
Open-source vector database popular in RAG pipelines.
Vector search engine for RAG applications.
Open-source vector database with cloud offering.
Meta's efficient similarity search library. Foundation for many RAG systems.
Rerank API and embed models for RAG pipelines.
Anthropic's 2024 blog post on contextual retrieval technique.
Extending RAG to include multimodal retrieval and generation. Survey exists (arXiv 2504.08748).
RAGAS, ARES, and other evaluation frameworks for RAG quality. Need verification.
Chunk after embedding with long-context encoder. From Jina AI. Details fuzzy.
Reference-free RAG evaluation framework. Metrics: faithfulness, contextual relevancy, answer relevancy, precision, recall. Open-source.
RAG evaluation tool alternative to RAGAS. Version 2.3 with advanced faithfulness metrics.
RAG evaluation and tracing tool for monitoring production RAG quality.
Embed all tokens first with long-context model, then chunk after transformer. No additional training. Superior to naive chunking. In jina-embeddings-v3 API.
Add context to each chunk using LLM before embedding. Makes chunks self-contained. More expensive than late chunking but preserves semantic coherence.
Vision language model approach to document retrieval for multimodal RAG. Uses visual features directly.
Programming-not-prompting framework from Stanford NLP. MIPRO v2 optimizer. RAG improvement from 53% to 61%. Lowest overhead ~3.53ms.
State-of-the-art multimodal embedding model. Late chunking in API. Outperforms OpenAI/Cohere on MTEB.
Leading commercial reranker. Cross-encoder architecture. 100+ language support. Nimble version for production.
Best overall reranker for production use per benchmarks. Ahead of Cohere Rerank v4 Pro.
Knowledge corruption attacks on RAG. Malicious documents in knowledge base mislead retrieval and generation.
Prompt injection via retrieved documents. Attack surface extends through SharePoint, Drive, Confluence.
Privacy risk: attackers can determine if specific documents are in the RAG knowledge base.
Emerging paradigm replacing simple RAG. Dynamically assembles optimal context per task. Hottest RAG topic late 2025.
Multiple competing eval frameworks (RAGAS, DeepEval, TruLens). No industry standard. Enterprise metrics differ from research metrics.
Extends RAG to image, audio, video retrieval. ColPali uses vision models. MRAG outperforms text-only RAG in visual+text scenarios.
RAGAS, DeepEval 2.3, TruLens, Phoenix, Maxim. Key metrics: faithfulness, context precision, answer relevancy.
